revision.text:

| architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}}

'''Haguro Shrine''' (羽黒神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Funabashi|en|Funabashi|qid=Q271417|lt=Funabashi|1=Funabashi}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, in the Higashi-Nakayama district. It serves as the tutelary shrine of Futago and is also referred to as {{ill|Haguro Gongen|ja|羽黒権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Gongen|qq=}}. It is believed that the shrine was relocated to its current location around the middle of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}.

This shrine is said to have been enshrined in the area by a monk of {{ill|Haguroyama|en|Haguroyama|qid=Q11610423|lt=Mount Haguro|1=Haguroyama}} after the members of the Futago Village Haguro Kōjū (a local pilgrimage group) made a pilgrimage to {{ill|Three Mountains of Dewa|en|Three Mountains of Dewa|qid=Q1155726|lt=Dewa Sanzan|1=Three Mountains of Dewa}}. However, the shrine is not directly related to the founding of Futago Village.

{{Shinto-stub}}

}}

{{translated page|ja|羽黒神社 (船橋市)|version=88377484|comment=Imported full ja history}}
